what_it_does: Defines the ui-contract entry for the agent-team resource, cataloging Claude Code's native subagent and experimental agent-team surfaces as planned company capabilities and routing the live parallel-worker functionality to the company session-fabric consult fan.
observations: Distinguishes three overlapping worker surfaces: native subagents, native agent teams, and the company session fabric.
Native surfaces are marked planned; the live parallel-agent capability is the consult fan on [[session#op: session.post]].
Includes a JSON schema for .claude/agents/*.md subagent definition frontmatter.
Provides a comparison table of native reality versus company reality as of 2026-06-12.
Defines two planned operations: agent-team.list (roster read) and agent-team.act (team control), both routing to fabric equivalents.
Documents native interaction semantics, permission inheritance hazards, and subagent tool restrictions.
Defines resource-level errors agent-team.not-exposed (501) and agent-team.experimental-disabled (409) with teach messages pointing to the consult fan.
Explicitly states there is no team:// scheme; native teams are name/path keyed and fabric fans are thread keyed.
Contains a synthetic example showing the planned error response.
Relates to session, session-message, model, permission, and headless-control.
standouts: It is a planned-surface catalog that repeatedly marks honest gaps rather than pretending native teams are exposed.
Every live parallel-agent need is explicitly routed to [[session#op: session.post]] verb=consult.
It documents the full native contract (schemas, semantics, gates) even though the company does not wrap it.
It highlights the behavioral mismatch between native teams (auto-messaging, shared task list) and fabric consult forks (isolated, durable mail).
conventions: Follows contract-entry frontmatter with type, resource, summary, schemes, status, and relates-to.
Uses contract:schema, contract:op, contract:error, and contract:example fenced blocks.
References related resources with [[...]] wiki-style links.
Distinguishes planned versus building/live status and uses live-twin routing.
Maintains neutral, non-ranking language and calls out gaps explicitly.
Sources native behavior to external Claude Code documentation URLs.
concerns: The detailed planned native request schema and operations could be mistaken for a live API unless the warnings are heeded.
Heavy reliance on external URLs that may drift as Claude Code docs evolve.
No actual code symbols are present; the file is entirely contract prose and schemas.
notes: A planned-capability contract that maps Claude Code's native multi-agent surfaces to the live session-fabric consult fan, with explicit 501/409 errors for unexposed control paths.
questions: When and under what conditions will the company layer expose native team or subagent control?
How exactly are native teammate names mapped to fabric session names like consult-<target>-N?
How does the fabric enforce fork-law byte-identical origins and the consult-fan cap?
What is the precise meaning of 'F1 session fabric' as the company's working team primitive?
What is the supervisor-http anonymous-local model referenced in the Caller section?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it catalogs native agent-team/subagent surfaces as planned and routes the working parallel-agent capability to the fabric.
apparent_themes: Honest gap documentation instead of feigning unavailable capabilities.
Clear separation of native Claude Code surfaces from company fabric primitives.
Contract-first specification with schemas, operations, and error vocabulary.
Safety awareness around permission inheritance and experimental gates.
Routing consumers to the live equivalent rather than the planned surface.
intention_signals: Prevent consumers from trying to drive native Claude Code agent teams or subagents through company APIs, while preserving a complete reference of those native surfaces and steering working use cases to the session-fabric consult fan.
novelty: It uses the contract format to document a native third-party feature set that the company explicitly does not expose, functioning as a 'negative capability' map with a live-twin routing pattern.
contribution: A crisp boundary between native Claude Code multi-agent surfaces and the company fabric's parallel-worker primitive; the routing rule that parallel agents today use session.post consult; the error vocabulary and teach messages for unexposed native-team attempts; and the native subagent definition schema and semantics as reference material.
summary_for_embedding: A planned ui-contract entry for agent-team that documents Claude Code's native subagents and experimental agent teams, states they are not exposed by the company layer, and routes live parallel-agent work to the session-fabric consult fan on session.post.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog native multi-agent surfaces and prevent misuse by directing all current parallel-agent needs to the existing session-fabric consult primitive.
